data.title: Safe Supply Streaming co appoints new finance chief; shares up
data.description:
• Safe Supply Streaming Co. (OTCQB:SSPLF) announced that effective December 3, 2024, Jordan Greenberg has been appointed the new chief financial officer.
  • Greenberg will succeed outgoing CFO Geoff Kritzinger.
  • SSPLF shares were up over 52% on Tuesday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GM recalls +132k heavy-duty pickup trucks to fix sudden tailgate unlatching issue
data.description:
General Motors (NYSE:GM) is recalling 132,037 heavy-duty pickup trucks in the U.S. for being equipped with power-unlatching tailgates, which could possibly lead to a road accident.

"Under certain circumstances, the tailgate design permits water to intrude into the gate, which, in combination with an unsealed switch, can result in a short circuit. Customers may notice the tailgate has inadvertently opened," the safety recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said.

The report said 66,669 Chevrolet Silverado 2500/3500 heavy-duty vehicles and 65,368 GMC Sierra 2500/3500 heavy-duty vehicles were affected by the recall.

The company said in the NHTSA report that it is aware of 237 field complaints of an unwanted tailgate opening while driving, which were received between September 22, 2023, and September 3, 2024.

"The gate may inadvertently unlatch while the vehicle is in Park. If the driver does not manually close the gate before beginning a drive, cargo in the bed of the truck may exit the vehicle and become a road hazard, increasing the risk of a crash," the recall report said.

The company said it is not aware of any alleged crashes or injuries associated with this issue and will fix it by replacing the exterior touchpad switch assemblies.

Vehicle owners will be notified about the recall schedule to fix the issue on Jan 13.

data.title: AMD, Nvidia, others in focus as Chinese chip associations call US processors 'unsafe'
data.description:
AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) and Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) were among the U.S. semiconductor companies were in focus on Tuesday as four Chinese semiconductor industry associations said processors made in the U.S. are "no longer safe" in response to the newly enacted export control curbs by the Biden administration.

AMD and Nvidia shares were little changed in early trading.

The comments came from groups including Internet Society of China and the China Association of Communication Enterprises. In total, the four groups, which released similarly worded statements right after each other, represent roughly 6,400 members combined. The statements did not say why U.S. processors are "unsafe" or unreliable, Reuters reported.

Nvidia, which reported third-quarter results late last month, said it saw data center revenue in China grow sequentially due to shipments of "export-compliant copper products to industries." AMD said it is "underrepresented" in China in the server CPU market and believes it may have an opportunity to gain share in the Asian country.

Intel (NASDAQ:INTC), which also has exposure to China, said it saw a 50% drop in shipments to the country in its most recent quarter.

Separately on Tuesday, China's commerce ministry banned exports of "dual-use items" related to gallium, germanium, antimony and super hard materials to the U.S. with immediate effect.

Gallium and germanium are used in semiconductors, while germanium is also used in infrared technology, fiber optic cables and solar cells.

data.title: Job openings rise more than expected in October; quits rate rises too: JOLTS
data.description:
• October Job Openings: 7.744M vs. 7.490M consensus and 7.443M prior.
  • Job openings rate: 4.6% vs. 4.5% prior.
  • Quits rate: 2.1% vs. 1.9% prior.
  • Breaking...check back for updates.

data.title: CleanSpark mines 622 bitcoins in November
data.description:
CleanSpark (NASDAQ:CLSK) mined 622 bitcoins in November, achieving daily production high of over 21 bitcoin.

That marks a fall from 655 bitcoins produced in October. Throughout the month, CleanSpark’s (CLSK) average hashrate was 32.32 EH/s and average fleet efficiency was 19.59 J/Th, resulting in an average of 20.72 bitcoin mined daily.

The company increased hashrate and efficiency month over month by over 7% and 6%, respectively. Its bitcoin holdings as of Nov 30 came in at 9,297.

"Our teams have been relentlessly executing, making progress towards our year-end hashrate goal of 37 EH/s while improving our efficiency," said CleanSpark CEO Zach Bradford. "Two turnkey bitcoin mining sites near Clinton, MS, have been completed and are expected to be fully operational providing an added 1 EH/s by the end of this week. We then expect expansion projects in Tennessee to go live by mid-month, that paired with our ongoing upgrades will bring us over our year-end target of 37 EH/s. We have our eyes on 2025, and our plan is to continue growing beyond 50 EH/s in the coming quarters."

CLSK shares were down around 9%.

data.title: Revelation Biosciences announces exercise of warrants for $4M in gross proceeds
data.description:
• Revelation Biosciences (REVB) entered into a definitive agreement for the immediate exercise of certain outstanding warrants to purchase up to an aggregate of 4.06M shares of common stock, issued by the company on August 22, 2024, at the exercise price of $1.00 per share. 
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about December 3, 2024.
  • REVB shares fell over 25% on Tuesday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eos Energy surges after closing $303.5M DoE loan guarantee
data.description:
Eos Energy Enterprises (NASDAQ:EOSE) +17.6% in early trading Tuesday after saying it closed a $303.5M loan guaranteed by the U.S. Department of Energy to advance Project American Made Zinc Energy, which is expected to fund the expansion of Eos Energy's (EOSE) manufacturing capacity to 8 GWh by 2027.

The closing marks the first Title 17 Battery loan closed under the Biden administration, following an application and approval process that began in January 2021.

"The DoE loan guarantee, alongside our strategic investment from Cerberus, is expected to provide the capital required to build a profitable manufacturing business and supports our progress as we execute the Project AMAZE roadmap," CFO Nathan Kroeker said.

data.title: Path forward for Ollie's Bargain Outlet grows trickier - analyst
data.description:
The best time to own Ollie’s Bargain Outlet Holdings (NASDAQ:OLLI) may have passed, said Wells Fargo analyst Edward Kelly, and with the path forward “trickier than appreciated” the profitability of the company seems to be getting tougher.

With the consumer backdrop evolving and the investment profile deteriorating, Kelly downgrades Ollie’s (OLLI) to Equal Weight from Overweight and cuts his target price by 5% to $95.

“The last 2.5+ years has probably been the best environment for OLLI’s business,” Kelly says, attributing the company’s success to the 2022 retail inventory glut which yielded “long tail product availability” when consumers prioritized value. But history says there are cycles and volatility inherent in the close-out model, and Kelly believes some added caution towards Ollie’s (OLLI) is now warranted.

Additionally, as competitor Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) liquidates, the risk to Ollie’s (OLLI) increases with consumers now flocking to the “extreme values” offered by Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ). This is generating more traffic towards Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) at Ollie’s expense, with the impact exacerbated by the fact that Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) is not going away as a competitor.

“As OLLI cycles out of the best time to run a close-out business, the road ahead could be trickier. We acknowledge there are still unique positives to consider in its below-average tariff exposure, and the BIG bankruptcy, [but] the retail investing landscape also remains a challenge, so these tailwinds are enticing, but we see other less favorable factors to consider,” Kelly says.

The downgrade is pressuring OLLI at the open, with the stock down ~4%.

data.title: Riot produces 495 Bitcoin in November 2024
data.description:
• Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) announced production of 495 Bitcoin in November 2024, compared to 552 in the same period last year.
  • Total Bitcoin held was 11,425, up 55% Y/Y.
  • Operating hash rate increased 13% month-over-month.
  • RIOT shares were down 2.3% on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press Release
